backend/main.mo: Main Motoko actor implementing video clip management with access control, including CRUD operations for clips and user profiles, trending analytics, similarity scoring, and role-based authorization for users and admins.
feature_evidence.json: Feature evidence report documenting debugging tasks for the Admin button visibility issue in the Layout component, focusing on authentication state handling, the useIsOwner hook, and conditional rendering logic.
frontend-file-summaries.txt: JSON-formatted summaries of all frontend source files describing their purpose, functionality, and key implementation details for the Beast Clipping application.
frontend/index.css: Global Tailwind CSS styles with custom OKLCH color system defining design tokens for light and dark modes, including primary, secondary, muted, destructive, and sidebar color variables.
frontend/index.html: HTML entry point for the React application with the title "Beast Clipping" and a root div for mounting the app.
frontend/src/App.tsx: Main React application component configuring TanStack Router with home and admin routes, managing video playback state, clip selection, AI suggestions, and admin access protection using Internet Identity authentication.
frontend/src/components/AdminErrorBounda: React error boundary component that catches and displays errors in the admin panel with detailed error messages, stack traces, and recovery options including retry and return home buttons.
frontend/src/components/AdminPanel.tsx: Admin dashboard component displaying platform statistics (total clips, trending clips, analytics) in cards and a table of trending clip analytics with retry functionality on error.
frontend/src/components/ClipCard.tsx: Card component displaying a video clip with thumbnail, title, duration, viral score badge, and delete functionality with confirmation dialog.
frontend/src/components/ClipList.tsx: Displays a scrollable list of saved video clips using ClipCard components, with loading states and empty state messaging.
Shows either AI-generated clip suggestions or trending viral clips with viral scores, allowing users to select suggestions to populate the clip creation form.
frontend/src/components/ClipTimestampCon: Form component for creating video clips with timestamp inputs (start/end), title field, and save functionality that accepts suggested values from AI or selected clips.
frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx: Main layout wrapper with header containing branding, Internet Identity authentication button, and conditional admin panel link for authorized users.
frontend/src/components/VideoUrlForm.tsx: Form component for entering YouTube URLs with validation, video loading, and AI clip generation functionality. Displays error alerts and loading states for both URL submission and AI clip generation.
Badge component that displays viral potential scores with color-coded styling (green/amber/gray) and icons (flame/trending/sparkles) based on score thresholds. Supports multiple sizes and optional label display.
frontend/src/components/YouTubePlayer.ts: Simple YouTube video player component that embeds a YouTube iframe with responsive 16:9 aspect ratio using the provided video ID.
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts: React Query hook that manages the backend actor instance, creating authenticated or anonymous actors based on Internet Identity state. Invalidates dependent queries when the actor changes.
frontend/src/hooks/useAdminStats.ts: Hook that fetches admin statistics including total clips count and trending clip analytics from the backend actor using React Query.
Hook for AI-powered clip generation that currently uses mock data to suggest viral clips with timestamps and scores. Includes mutation logic to call backend and invalidate related queries on success.
frontend/src/hooks/useClipCreation.ts: Hook for creating video clips with a viral score calculation algorithm based on duration (15-60s optimal) and title characteristics. Uses React Query mutation to save clips via the backend actor.
frontend/src/hooks/useClips.ts: Hook for fetching all clips and deleting individual clips using React Query. Tracks deletion state and invalidates clip queries after successful deletion.
frontend/src/hooks/useClipTimestamps.ts: Hook managing clip timestamp state (start/end minutes/seconds) with validation, formatting, and conversion utilities. Provides methods to check validity, format time ranges, and set timestamps from seconds.
frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.t: React context and hook for Internet Identity authentication, managing login/logout flow, identity state, and AuthClient lifecycle. Provides status tracking and automatic identity restoration from local storage.
frontend/src/hooks/useIsOwner.ts: React hook that queries the backend to check if the current authenticated user is an admin, with extensive logging for debugging authentication state.
frontend/src/hooks/useTrendingClips.ts: React hook that fetches trending video clips from the backend using React Query when the actor is available.
Custom hook providing utility functions to validate YouTube URLs and extract video IDs using regex patterns.
frontend/src/index.css: Tailwind CSS configuration with custom CSS variables for light and dark theme colors using OKLCH color space.
frontend/src/main.tsx: Application entry point that sets up React Query and Internet Identity providers, then renders the App component.
frontend/src/utils/urlParams.ts: Utility functions for extracting, persisting, and managing URL parameters from both regular query strings and hash-based routing, with sessionStorage support.
frontend/tailwind.config.js: Tailwind CSS configuration defining custom theme colors, container settings, animations, and plugins for the application's design system.
project_state.json: Project metadata tracking completed features, known issues (including admin panel authentication problems), and architectural notes about access control changes.
